local_witnesses_first.why: Battlewrath, 2026-07-17: 'a lot of the state reading is already well proven in weakaura/engine/sheets. It already has well defined documentation on target, unit tokens and the like.' The sheets model exists so reasoning READS instead of re-deriving, and a LOCAL sourced witness (the installed fork's own Types.lua) beats any retail doc. Local witnesses are joined FIRST.
local_witnesses_first.NOT_redundant_with_the_wikis: Battlewrath, 2026-07-17: 'This is discovery. Using our internal data is just leveraging the investments, not an error.' Correct, and worth stating precisely: the sheets carry ZERO conditionals (42 of the candidates are flags), OMIT `mouseover` (the most-used macro target), and carry no patch dating - they could not have answered the question. Overlap = 11 unit tokens, where they are the STRONGER witness. COMPLEMENTARY, not redundant: the JOIN is the value, and the one sharp find (@nameplate live-proven WITH boundary semantics) needed both sources on the table.
local_witnesses_first.wa_sheets: Weak Auras/engine/Fact_basis/sheets/domains.json - baseUnitId / multiUnitId, extracted from the INSTALLED fork's Types.lua. A LOCAL SOURCED witness on THIS client; strictly stronger than a retail wiki.
local_witnesses_first.but_a_DIFFERENT_question: WA's list is what WA OFFERS AS A TRIGGER UNIT, not the client's token vocabulary. It ADDS abstractions that are not client tokens, and OMITS tokens WA has no use for (mouseover - WA watches state, it does not cast). LISTING corroborates; OMISSION proves nothing.

wa_only_not_client_tokens.group: WA aggregation abstraction, NOT a client unit token - never a macro target
wa_only_not_client_tokens.grouppets: WA aggregation abstraction, NOT a client unit token - never a macro target
wa_only_not_client_tokens.grouppetsonly: WA aggregation abstraction, NOT a client unit token - never a macro target
wa_only_not_client_tokens.partypets: WA aggregation abstraction, NOT a client unit token - never a macro target
wa_only_not_client_tokens.partypetsonly: WA aggregation abstraction, NOT a client unit token - never a macro target

what: THE QUESTION REGISTER - every candidate conditional + its PROOF MARK. This is the probe's ask-list, not a fact table.
NOT_A_FACT_SOURCE: Nothing here is a fact about the CoA client. Every entry is a QUESTION until a probe verdict replaces its proof_mark. Do not cite reference/ as basis/.
standing_and_why_tracked: Battlewrath, 2026-07-17: 'Track them. Their reference material, not authority. Just as much as the corpus is reference. They help fill curated intent.' SAME STANDING AS corpus/: reference feeds INTENT, never authority. corpus/ harvests how people build auras; this harvests how people write macros. Two uses, and the second is easy to miss: (1) the ask-list - the only non-invented source for the probe's questions; (2) CURATED INTENT - the worked examples (smart-heal fallback chains, mouseover healing, modifier swaps) are what players actually WANT a macro to do, which is design input for the guide, the same role a corpus/patterns entry plays for auras. Raws are committed VERBATIM (the opposite call from the gitignored client study copy, deliberately): an extractor can regenerate the client; a wiki fetch CANNOT - the page moves and the revid with it. The raw IS the reproducibility.
why_allowed: Recall and secondary sources are inadmissible as a FACT source but admissible as a QUESTION source, because the probe adjudicates. A wrong candidate costs one probe row reading 'unsupported'. A wrong fact poisons the basis silently.
absence_proves_nothing: A conditional no wiki lists is UN-ASKED, not unsupported. Demonstrated mechanically: ['@pettarget'] witnessed in this client's own Lua and in NO wiki.

proof_marks.UNPROVEN: claimed by a secondary source; NO witness of any kind on this client. A question, nothing more.
proof_marks.LOCAL-SOURCE-CORROBORATED: the WA sheets (sourced from the INSTALLED fork's Types.lua on THIS client) list this unit. Stronger than a retail wiki. WA's OMISSION of a token proves nothing - different use case.
proof_marks.LOCALLY-PROVEN (macro-targeting still op: this project already LIVE-PROVED the token resolves (see local_proven). Whether a MACRO can target it is a separate, still-open question.
proof_marks.SOURCE-CORROBORATED: the client's own Lua witnesses the token (a handler compares the parser's returned target to it). Handler-side only, and target tokens only - never flags. Still wants the probe.
proof_marks.PROVEN-TRUE: probe: known and currently true. [PROBE WRITES THIS]
proof_marks.PROVEN-FALSE: probe: known and currently false. [PROBE WRITES THIS]
proof_marks.UNSUPPORTED: probe: parser rejects both polarities. [PROBE WRITES THIS]
proof_marks.IGNORED: probe: both polarities true => no-op. [PROBE WRITES THIS]
proof_marks.LIVE:SUPPORTED-PROVEN: the capture: varies across contexts - it tracked real state
proof_marks.LIVE:SUPPORTED: the capture: reads FALSE somewhere, so the parser evaluated it
proof_marks.LIVE:SUPPORTED-TRUE: the capture: constant true, corroborated by a witness or a false arg-sibling
proof_marks.LIVE:UNSUPPORTED-IGNORED: the capture: the parser IGNORES it (clause passes). Proven by a witness contradiction or an absent subsystem.
proof_marks.LIVE:AMBIGUOUS: the capture could not decide, and the map does NOT guess. On this parser an ignored clause is indistinguishable from a true one.
proof_marks.LIVE:RESOLVES: the capture: UnitExists() true for this token
proof_marks.LIVE:DID-NOT-RESOLVE-HERE: the capture: no unit in these 5 contexts. NOT absence - context-bound. Unproven, not unsupported.
proof_marks.LIVE:NOT-A-UNIT: not a unit token at all (cursor: a macro-layer special case)

two_mechanisms.why_this_matters: `@unit` and `[flag]` are NOT the same mechanism and do NOT share a proof method. Conflating them is the easiest way to write a confident wrong guide.

@unit / target=.mechanism: HYPOTHESIS: a pass-through STRING. SecureCmdOptionParse appears to return whatever follows @; support lives DOWNSTREAM in the handler's own special-casing (/cast checks target=='cursor' -> Custom_HandleTerrainClick) and in unit resolution.
@unit / target=.independent_support: wowpedia/Macro_conditionals states '@unitId : Replace with any valid unitId' and '`target=` is also an alias for `@`' - a RETAIL doc describing the same pass-through shape the CoA handler code implies. Two directions, one hypothesis. Still a hypothesis.
@unit / target=.consequence: the polarity matrix does NOT apply; there is likely no [no@cursor].
@unit / target=.probe_row_that_settles_it: SecureCmdOptionParse("[@banana] X") - if a nonsense unit returns target="banana", @ is pass-through and the vocabulary question does not apply to it at all.
@unit / target=.status: HYPOTHESIS - not asserted. One probe row settles it.

[flag].mechanism: actually EVALUATED by the C parser (combat, mod:shift, group, ...).
[flag].consequence: THIS is the vocabulary question, and what the polarity matrix is for.

the_cursor_collision.why_this_matters: THREE different mechanisms collide on the word 'cursor'. They are not variants of each other and none became another. Conflating any two writes a confidently wrong guide.

@cursor.is: a ground LOCATION - the terrain point under the pointer. NOT a unit: it appears in NO unit-token list on either wiki.
@cursor.on_CoA: hand-rolled by Ascension - /cast checks target=='cursor' and hands off to Custom_HandleTerrainClick. A CONFIRMED, DATED backport.

@mouseover.is: a UNIT - 'the unit which the mouse is currently (or was most recently) hovering over'. A real unit token; resolves via UnitExists.
@mouseover.dated: UNDATED in every source we hold. The macro SYSTEM itself dates to 2.0.1 (sourced), so mouseover-as-a-macro-target is >= 2.0.1 - but that is a floor, not a date. We do not know when it shipped.

[cursor].is: a FLAG - 'what the cursor is currently HOLDING' (drag-and-drop state; GetCursorInfo). Neither a location nor a unit.

the_cursor_collision.the_history_read: Battlewrath, 2026-07-17: 'the cursor is first a location selector, and then later became mouseover casting.' RIGHT about the mechanic, inverted about the token. The ground RETICLE is the older thing - the Ascension wiki frames @cursor as 'remove the reticle delay', so the reticle pre-existed and @cursor AUTOMATES it. But as macro TOKENS the order reverses: @mouseover is undated and old (>= the 2.0.1 macro system), @cursor is 7.1.0 - about nine years later. Sequence: reticle (location, pre-macro) -> 2.0 macro system brings mouseover casting -> @cursor (7.1.0) arrives late to automate the ORIGINAL location role. They are parallel, not a lineage.
the_cursor_collision.status: the DATES are sourced; the SEQUENCE above is a reading of them, not a fact claim. Nothing here changes what the probe asks.

era_signal_legend.patch-dated: SOURCED - the wiki carries a {{Patch}} annotation (often with a Blizzard citation). EVIDENCE. If the patch is > 3.x it postdates the 3.3.5a base => present only if BACKPORTED.
era_signal_legend.archive-documented: in the wowwiki archive (content ~2010, WotLK/early-Cata) => expected present on a 3.3.5a base
era_signal_legend.retail-documented-only: retail wiki lists it, the ~2010 archive does not, and NO patch annotation exists => UNDATED. We do NOT know when it shipped. Absence is not evidence.
era_signal_legend.ascension-claimed: only the Ascension wiki says so
era_signal_legend.NOT-IN-ANY-WIKI: only this client's own source says so

era_method_correction: era_signal originally called this bucket 'post-wotlk', inferring the history from ABSENCE in the ~2010 archive - the exact 'absence proves nothing' error this slice writes into three files, committed by the tool built to prevent it, in the field the probe steers by. It was RIGHT for the 4 conditionals carrying patch annotations - by LUCK, not method. Now: a patch annotation is evidence and dates the row; absence yields only 'retail-documented-only' = UNDATED.

ascension-wiki-macros.standing: SECONDARY - the server's own guide; inherits its conditional list from the retail wikis below (see its Resources section)

wowwiki-archive-making-a-macro.standing: SECONDARY (retail) - frozen 2018, documents spell RANKS => WotLK-era; closest external match to a 3.3.5a client. Carries a COMPLETENESS CLAIM for ITS era, which is not a claim about CoA.

wowpedia-making-a-macro.standing: SECONDARY (retail) - cited by the Ascension wiki; delegates its conditional list to Macro_conditionals

wowpedia-macro-conditionals.standing: SECONDARY (retail) - the delegation target of the cited page

warcraft-wiki-unittoken.standing: SECONDARY (retail, CURRENT) - Wowpedia's live successor (moved off fandom 2023). The maintained unit-token reference.

wowpedia-unitid.standing: SECONDARY (retail) - the frozen fandom copy of the same reference; the pair brackets the era the way the macro wikis do.

completeness_check.what: candidates missing era_signal / probe_method / probe_rows. MUST be empty. A branch-skip left probe_method NULL on 15 candidates while the run reported success - twice, in the same function. An empty list here is the positive check that a difference-diff cannot give you.

grammar_claims.claim: clauses evaluate in order; the FIRST true one wins
`@unit`/`target=` is resolved FIRST, before the other conditionals
`@unit` also sets the unit the OTHER conditionals are checked against
`@unit`/`target=` is resolved FIRST, before the other conditionals
`@unitId` takes ANY valid unitId => supports the PASS-THROUGH hypothesis

SUFFIX GRAMMAR: append `target` to ANY unit token, repeatable (pettarget, playertargettarget). This DERIVES tokens rather than listing them - which is exactly why no wiki lists @pettarget as a base token, and why its absence from every list proves nothing.
unit tokens are CASE INSENSITIVE (consistent with CoA's handler doing target:lower() == 'cursor')
a party/raid member's NAME works as a unit, with hyphens for the chain (Cogwheel-target-target)
unit tokens are CASE INSENSITIVE (consistent with CoA's handler doing target:lower() == 'cursor')
a party/raid member's NAME works as a unit, with hyphens for the chain (Cogwheel-target-target)

local_proven.status: LIVE-PROVEN on this client: nameplateN unit tokens EXIST and RESOLVE.
local_proven.evidence: corpus/patterns/guardian-health-tracker.md - the guardian-tracker POC resolves summoned GUIDs to nameplate1..N tokens and reads UnitHealth off them. LIVE-PROVEN 2026-07-15 (four bars). Evidence pair: addons/landing/records/20260715_195725_117__plates.json (plates on -> tokens + real HP) / ...195846_424__plates.json (rendered, plates unpopulated -> no tokens).
local_proven.richer_than_a_probe: it carries the BOUNDARY too - the token's lifetime = PLATE POPULATION, not model visibility. A one-shot UnitExists read learns far less.
local_proven.what_remains_open: reading STATE via a plate token (PROVEN) and TARGETING one FROM A MACRO are DIFFERENT questions. The wiki states 'nameplateN cannot be targeted by spells or commands such as /cast [target=nameplate1]' - UNVERIFIED on CoA. The row survives with a sharper question behind it.

This client backports freely, and @cursor (7.1.0) is a CONFIRMED backport witnessed in its own source - so presence is a real question, not a formality.

live_verdict.why: UnitExists('nameplate1') true in: ['rest', 'spell-form', 'stealth', 'combat', 'mounted']

claimed_by.says: PvP talents are usable

pvpcombat.era_note: SOURCED: added in patch 7.3.0 (Legion). The CoA client is 3.3.5a (WotLK), so this POSTDATES its base => present here only if BACKPORTED. This client backports freely, and @cursor (7.1.0) is a CONFIRMED backport witnessed in its own source - so presence is a real question, not a formality.

live_verdict.why: reads TRUE in every context, and the SUBSYSTEM it names is absent from this client (census: no API matching /PvpTalent|PVPTalent|PvPTalent/). the wiki defines it as 'PvP talents are usable' and dates it 7.3.0 (Legion). PvP TALENTS are a Legion system - distinct from PVP itself, which this client has in full (instanced, duels, flagged combat: UnitIsPVP / GetBattlefieldStatus / StartDuel all resident). If no PvP-talent API exists, the conditional names a system that is not here. Under proven ignore-semantics, a conditional naming a system that is not here and reading constant-TRUE is being ignored.
